vitals.date_specific: 2026: December 5 (per ASBRA calendar; organizer page still shows 2025)
vitals.start_format: 7:00 a.m. start (mass start documented in 2019); open roads under normal traffic rules
vitals.feed_zones: Hydration/rest stops, food zones, bike support, tail vehicle

route_options.name: Full (West Maui Loop)
route_options.notes: Organizer advertises '100 km / 60 miles' (labels not exact conversions; a 2025 participant logged 63.37 mi) with 4,173 ft of climbing; e-bikes permitted

climate.description: Tropical Maui in December: warm and humid with trade winds; exposed coastal sections and rain showers possible on the windward north side.
challenges: Humidity and sun exposure on the coastal sections
Wet, slick corners on the narrow northern roads after showers

terrain.surface: Paved roads (Kahekili Highway sections narrow, rough, partly single-lane)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 71. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67.

course_description.character: A loop of the West Maui Mountains out of Kahului — the West Maui Loop, passing through Lahaina and Kahakuloa. This is rolling, punchy coastal riding rather than one long mountain pass: the organizer lists 4,173 ft of gain over the full route, with a few short, steep climbs in the remote northern section, where the Kahekili Highway narrows to rough, sometimes single-lane pavement with little shoulder (the loop's best-known steep pitch is locally nicknamed 'Mr. Steepy'). Roads stay open to traffic — riders keep right, obey normal traffic law, and need to be done by noon. Participant footage from 2023 and 2024 describes the short steep northern climbs, narrow-road sections, and a crash on a wet, tight corner late in the ride.
course_description.signature_challenge: The remote northern Kahekili Highway stretch: short, steep ramps on narrow, rough, partly single-lane pavement with jungle and ocean exposure — the technical and physical crux of the West Maui Loop.
suffering_zones: Northern West Maui section around Kahakuloa: short, steep climbs on narrow, rough pavement — a 2024 participant crashed on a wet, tight corner late in the ride here.
Final climb around mile 52: a 2023 participant video describes one last significant ascent before the run back to Kahului.

logistics.airport: Kahului Airport (OGG), minutes from the start
logistics.lodging_strategy: Stay in Kahului/Wailuku near the start-finish; the 2025 edition started and finished at Imua Family Services, 161 S. Wakea Avenue, Kahului.
logistics.transport: Supported charity ride: hydration/rest stops, food, mechanical support and a tail vehicle; post-ride brunch at the finish.
logistics.food: Aid-station food on course plus a post-ride brunch.

history.origin_story: Launched December 1, 2019 as the inaugural Pedal IMUA Gran Fondo, a fundraising ride for Imua Family Services' Dream IMUA program supporting children in crisis on Maui. About 100 riders registered for the first edition. The event's edition numbering has been inconsistent in its own announcements (both 2024 and 2025 were billed as the 'sixth annual'), but the 2019 founding is well documented in contemporary Maui press.
notable_moments: 2019: inaugural ride around the West Maui Mountains, ~100 riders (Maui News)
2025: sixth edition held December 6 out of Kahului with nearly 100 cyclists (Maui Now road advisory)
history.reputation: A small, well-supported charity fondo with genuine local backing — known for the West Maui Loop scenery and the Imua Family Services cause, not for competition.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Charity Ride on Maui's Best-Known Loop
biased_opinion.summary: Pedal IMUA is a small, supported charity ride around the West Maui Loop — roughly 100 riders, a 7:00 a.m. start, open roads under normal traffic rules, and everything wrapped around fundraising for Imua Family Services' Dream IMUA program. Be clear about what it isn't: the published materials emphasize support, fundraising and the post-ride brunch rather than timing, categories or ranked results, and e-bikes are welcome on both the 60-mile and 30-mile options. What it has is the route. The West Maui Loop is one of Maui's best-known road rides, and its remote northern stretch on the Kahekili Highway — narrow, rough, partly single-lane, with short steep pitches and a documented participant crash on a wet corner — gives the day real teeth. Note the organizer's own page has run stale (still showing the 2025 edition well into 2026), and third-party calendars disagree on the exact 2026 date (December 5 vs December 6), which is sloppy for an event asking you to book flights to an island.
strengths: The West Maui Loop itself — Lahaina, Kahakuloa, jungle and ocean scenery on one of Maui's best-known road rides
Genuinely well supported for its size: rest stops, food, mechanical support, a tail vehicle and a post-ride brunch
Real, verifiable charitable purpose — proceeds fund Imua Family Services' Dream IMUA program for children in crisis
Low-key and welcoming: all ages, e-bikes permitted, a 30-mile half option
weaknesses: Not a race in any meaningful sense — open roads, normal traffic rules, no ranked results in the published materials; competitive riders will find nothing to race
The organizer's own event page runs stale (still advertising the prior year's edition), and 2026 date listings conflict across calendars (Dec 5 vs Dec 6)
The northern Kahekili Highway section is narrow, rough and partly single-lane — a participant crash on a wet, tight corner is on video
Small field (~100 riders) and a long trip for an untimed 60-mile ride — the destination, not the event, has to justify the travel
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Ride it if you'll be on Maui in December and want the West Maui Loop with aid stations and a good cause attached. Fly there for it only if the destination, not the event, is the point — this is a supported charity ride, not a competitive gran fondo.

final_verdict.one_liner: A small, supported Maui charity ride on the West Maui Loop — well-known route, real cause, no race.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes as a supported way to ride the West Maui Loop for a good cause; no if you want timing, competition or closed roads — none of those exist here.
final_verdict.alternatives: Haleakala/Cycle to the Sun (also on Maui, an actual timed hillclimb) if you want competition; Aloha Gravel (same charity, prior weekend) if you want the off-road version.

videos.title: Pedal Imua Gran Fondo 2023 (West Maui Loop)
videos.description:
The Pedal Imua Gran Fondo is an annual 60-mile charity event that navigates you around the famed West Maui loop.

Here's footage from my participation in the December 2nd event.


ROUTE
• RidewithGPS: https://shorturl.at/elCZ7

EQUIP
• iPhone 13 Pro
• GoPro Hero 11
• Evoc Bike Bag Pro

BIKE
• S-works / Tarmac SL7
• • • Crank: 48/35t
• • • Gear ratio: 10-33t
• • • Wheels: Roval CLX50
• • • Tires: Goodyear Eagle F1

videos.title: Pedal Imua Gran Fondo Event 2022
videos.description:
This famed, scenic Gran Fondo ride around Maui's west side benefits Dream Imua, a wish granting program for children in crisis within Maui County.

🔗 For more info 👉🏾 pedalimua.com

videos.title: Pedal Imua - A Benefit Ride on Maui Hawaii
videos.description: Get ready for the first annual Pedal Imua, a benefit ride for children with special needs.  This 100k ride will circumvent the West Maui Mountains and raise funds for Dream Imua, a program for children in crisis.

quotes.text: The route is 60 miles with approximately 4,000 ft of climbing there's a few short steep climbs in the northern part of the route but for the most part nothing too serious and you're going around mountain passes, you've got ocean views, you go through jungle terrain.

I just wiped out and crashed just about 5 miles back on a wet tight corner, I didn't get hurt too bad just kind of scraped up my kit and my knee but all things considered this is a really beautiful ride.

key_challenges.name: Final final climb
key_challenges.description: The last major climb around mile 52, described as the final challenge before the finish.

key_challenges.name: Wet tight corners
key_challenges.description: Wet, tight corners that can cause crashes around mile 47, with one rider wiping out on a wet corner.

key_challenges.name: Short steep climbs
key_challenges.description: A few short steep climbs in the northern part of the route, though described as nothing too serious overall.

terrain_notes.text: Some sections where the road is pretty narrow, going around mountain passes with ocean views and jungle terrain.
Wet tight corners that can be slippery and cause crashes, especially later in the route.

additional_quotes.text: I managed to beat my time from last year by 5 minutes flat which is weird considering that last year I had a blowout and I was off the bike for at least 30 minutes dealing with fixing the tire issue

This event is absolutely incredible so I highly recommend it for anyone looking to do an island style cycling Venture but also the event supports an important charitable cause for children and families in crisis
The event starts at the UA Discovery Garden in the town of wuku - it's an incredible looking estate really like where the event is held

rider_intel.search_text: Pedal IMUA is a 60-mile paved charity road event in Maui historically starting at UA Discovery Garden in Wailuku (2025 start moved to Kahului) with approximately 4,000 feet of climbing. The route features mountain passes with ocean views and jungle terrain, though some sections have narrow roads. Riders face a few short steep climbs in the northern section and a significant final climb around mile 52. Wet, tight corners pose crash risks, particularly later in the route around mile 47. The event supports Maui Family Services' Dream IMUA program for children and families in crisis. One experienced rider completed the course in 4 hours despite dealing with tire issues. The route offers stunning island scenery but requires caution on technical sections with narrow roads and potentially slippery corners.

eligibility.notes: Active. Most recent confirmed edition: December 6, 2025 (Maui Now road advisory; Stable Road Foundation recap). ASBRA and Tradewind Cycling Team calendars list Saturday December 5, 2026 in Kahului; Gran Fondo World Tour says December 6 (a Sunday) and the organizer's own page still shows the 2025 edition — 2026 date treated as listed-but-organizer-unconfirmed. VITALS CORRECTIONS (2026-07-24 editorial wave 5): elevation corrected from 5,800 ft/1,768 m (unsupported) to organizer-published 4,173 ft/1,272 m; distance aligned to the organizer's advertised 100 km/60 mi with a 50 km/30 mi half option added; founded corrected from 2016 to 2019 (inaugural edition Dec 1, 2019 per Maui Now/Maui News — 2016 likely confused with the older Paddle IMUA event); field size ~100 riders per 2019/2023/2025 reports; start venue now Kahului (was Wailuku in early editions). One YouTube entry (Conor O'Brian 190-mile solo fundraiser) purged as not being footage of this event. Verified via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research + curl of ASBRA calendar.
